coaching.title: Back-to-Back Day 2 — 11 mi
coaching.message: This is the money session: 11 miles on legs that are already trashed from yesterday. Keep it strictly easy, Zone 1-2 (cap HR near 138, well under your 165 LTHR). On every uphill, power hike — no running the climbs today. That's exactly how you'll move through the Julian Alps' 2,500m of vert. Target the ~1,500ft of gain on tired legs and treat it as your mile-25+ rehearsal. You'll feel rough early; settle in and keep moving. Antigua sits near 1,500m, so the thinner air is a free altitude bonus — respect it and don't chase pace.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Glycogen is depleted from yesterday, so prime before you go: 40-60g carbs an hour out (banana + toast or a gel with coffee). On the run, take 60-80g carbs/hr from the first 30 min — don't wait to feel low. This is gut training as much as leg training, so practice the exact products you'll race in Slovenia. Within 30 min of finishing: 25-30g protein plus carbs (3:1 carb:protein) to slam the recovery window shut.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: After a B2B block, prioritize getting horizontal and legs up for 10-15 min once you're refueled. Rehydrate with electrolytes — Guatemala heat plus altitude will have cost you more sodium than it feels like. Easy mobility for hips and calves, light foam roll on quads, and aim for 8+ hours of sleep tonight. The adaptation from this block banks over the next 48 hours, so guard your sleep.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Week 10 of 22, 87 days out — you're in the thick of the Build, and this is the work that makes the back half of a 50K feel survivable. The discomfort you'll feel around mile 6 today is the same discomfort you'll meet at km 35 in the Alps. Practice the response now: shoulders down, shorten the stride, keep the feet moving. You're not training to feel good — you're training to keep going when you don't.
